slides.title: 06_ICMP Error Handling for VPNs in SRv6 Networks: draft-varhal-6man-icmp-srv6-vpn

chat.author: Esko Dijk
chat.text: First WG today with an extensive QR code tutorial :)

chat.author: Éric Vyncke
chat.text: I guess that Zafar talks about the VTN-id ?

chat.author: Alan Jowett
chat.text:
Windows laptop works fine on ipv6 mostly, with one minor quirk:
Windows reports "No internet" despite seemingly internet reachable. Investigating and will see if I can determine why it reports "no internet"

chat.author: Esko Dijk
chat.text: @Behcet  Oh, I meant  the extensive explanation how to scan the QR code to join the session in datatracker.

chat.author: Éric Vyncke
chat.text: There is a liaison between IEEE and IETF, which works pretty fine with a synch up call between leadership 3 times/year

chat.author: Jerome Henry
chat.text: Yes, DMS allows for many seconds

chat.author: Ted Lemon
chat.text: FWIW I think it's a good idea, just hadn't thought to go read it, but definitely could.

chat.author: Jen Linkova
chat.text: Ted: the chairs would appreciate if you could review!

chat.text: (Apologies, I had to session hop over into IDR)

chat.author: Dave Thaler
chat.text: +1 to remove 7050 (really the principle I'd want is to avoid referencing anything that is just MAY)

chat.author: Dave Thaler
chat.text:
It talks about "The last option is to place the DNS64 function in the end hosts,
   coupled to the local (stub) resolver."

chat.author: Éric Vyncke
chat.text: FYI, on Thursday OPS Area, Ron is presenting all the drafts related to ICMP: "Proposals for ICMP Extensions: Operational Motivations & Rationale "

chat.author: Éric Vyncke
chat.text: Thanks to the chairs and to all
